title: Tennis Star Carlos Alcaraz Will Miss French Open Due to Wrist Injury
summary:
Have you ever had a wrist injury that kept you from playing your favorite sport? Imagine being Carlos Alcaraz, one of the best tennis players in the world, and having to miss the French Open because of a hurt wrist.

Alcaraz, 22, hurt his right wrist during a match in Barcelona last week. He had tests, and the doctors said he needs to be careful. So he pulled out of two big clay-court tournaments: the Italian Open and the French Open (also called Roland Garros).

This is a big deal because Alcaraz won the French Open the last two years! He was the defending champion. He also won the Australian Open this year, becoming the youngest man ever to win all four major tennis tournaments (the career Grand Slam).

"This is a difficult time for me," Alcaraz posted on Instagram, "but I am sure we will come out of this stronger."

His biggest rival, Jannik Sinner, said he was sad to hear the news. "Hopefully he can be back for Wimbledon," Sinner said. Sinner just beat Alcaraz in the Monte Carlo final and took over the world number one ranking. Now Sinner has a chance to win the French Open and complete his own career Grand Slam.

Alcaraz has only missed one Grand Slam tournament since 2021 — the 2023 Australian Open because of a hamstring injury. Let's hope he heals quickly and returns to the court soon!
why_it_matters: This shows that even professional athletes have to take breaks when they are hurt. It's important to listen to your body and rest when needed, just like in school sports.

keywords.explanation: A 22-year-old Spanish tennis star and defending French Open champion.
A major tennis tournament played on clay in Paris, also called Roland Garros.
The injury to his right wrist that forced Alcaraz to miss the tournament.
Alcaraz won the French Open the last two years, so he was defending his title.
Winning all four major tennis tournaments at least once. Alcaraz achieved this by winning the Australian Open.
Alcaraz's biggest rival, who is now world number one and has a chance to win the French Open.

Which tournament did Alcaraz have to miss besides the French Open?

What does Alcaraz's biggest rival Jannik Sinner hope?
options: He hopes Alcaraz can be back for Wimbledon
He hopes to beat Alcaraz again
questions.correct_answer: He hopes Alcaraz can be back for Wimbledon

questions.question: Which injury caused Alcaraz to miss the 2023 Australian Open?

background_read: The French Open is one of the four most important tennis tournaments in the world, played on clay courts in Paris.
Top players often get injured because the sport is very intense on the body.
Carlos Alcaraz is a young Spanish tennis star who became world number one at age 19.
Article_Structure: WHAT: He is missing the French Open due to a wrist injury.
WHERE: He hurt his wrist in Barcelona; he will miss tournaments in Italy and France.
WHY: Doctors said he needs to be careful to avoid more damage.

perspectives.description: He is upset about missing the tournament but is optimistic he will come back stronger.
He is also sad for his rival but sees an opportunity to win the French Open himself.
They are disappointed not to see their favorite player compete at a major tournament.
